derivatives.name: hy_ig_spread_bps
derivatives.definition: The raw HY-IG credit spread in basis points. A higher number means investors demand more yield to hold high-yield bonds relative to investment-grade bonds — a classic stress signal.
derivatives.formula: raw column (BAML HY OAS - BAML IG OAS), unit = bps
derivatives.notes: Sign convention: up = wider spread = more stress. Monthly series 1997+.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_zscore_lb252
derivatives.definition: The 252-day rolling z-score of the HY-IG spread. It tells you how unusual the current spread is relative to its own recent history.
derivatives.notes: Used as a threshold-ready standardized signal. Lookback aligns with OOS window.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_roc_63d
derivatives.definition: The 63-day (one quarter) rate of change of the HY-IG spread. It captures recent momentum — is the spread widening or tightening?

derivatives.name: hy_ig_hmm_2state_prob_stress
derivatives.definition: The probability that the HY-IG series is in the high-stress regime according to a 2-state Hidden Markov Model. Values near 1 mean the model is confident we are in a stressed regime.
derivatives.formula: Posterior P(regime=stress | spread_{1..t}) from Gaussian HMM(K=2)
derivatives.notes: Regime label assigned by variance ranking; state 0 = calm, state 1 = stress.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_pctrank_1260d
derivatives.definition: The 5-year percentile rank of the HY-IG spread. A value of 0.95 means the current spread is wider than 95% of the last five years of observations.
derivatives.notes: Alternative threshold input for P1_long_cash strategy. Values in [0, 1].

derivatives.name: hy_ig_vol_ann_21d
derivatives.definition: The 21-day annualized volatility of the HY-IG spread's daily change. It captures how choppy the spread has been recently.
derivatives.notes: Considered as a possible overlay but not rendered on production charts. Kept in scope for diagnostic transparency per ECON-UD.

derivatives.name: spy_close
derivatives.definition: The raw adjusted close price of the SPY ETF. All target-axis derivatives are computed from this single series.
derivatives.notes: Adjusted for dividends and splits. Daily series 1993+.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_return_21d
derivatives.definition: The forward 21-day (roughly one month) total return of SPY. Used to see whether a signal today predicts returns over the next month.
derivatives.notes: Primary evaluation horizon. Forward-looking — leakage-safe by construction.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_return_63d
derivatives.definition: The forward 63-day (one quarter) total return of SPY. A secondary horizon used to test whether a signal's predictive power decays with horizon.

derivatives.name: spy_drawdown_path
derivatives.definition: The running drawdown of SPY from its trailing all-time high. Used to see how signals behave as drawdowns deepen.
derivatives.notes: Non-positive by construction. Used in Strategy-page drawdown panel.

notes: Reference-instance example for ECON-SD / ECON-UD (Wave 7A). Mirrors the HY-IG v2 pair structure the stakeholder will re-audit in Wave 7B. Populated with realistic derivatives; not the live production registry.
